artworks.title: Classic Beauty
artworks.description: Classic Beauty is an acrylic on canvas measuring 50 × 76 cm, painted in 2010 — one of the earlier works in Walt Viviers' portfolio. The title and vertical format suggest a figurative subject approached with classical intent, rendered in Viviers' characteristically direct and confident style. At a stage when the Pretoria-based South African artist was developing the bold brushwork and clear-eyed observation that would come to define his mature practice, Classic Beauty reads as a thoughtful study in portraiture — grounded in tradition but with a contemporary sensibility. The generous scale gives the subject room to breathe and rewards close inspection. The original is available for acquisition.

artworks.title: Dragon Kitties
artworks.description: Dragon Kitties is a small-format acrylic on canvas measuring 15 × 15 cm, completed in 2025. The piece brings together two of Walt Viviers' recurring interests — animals and the fantastical — in a compact, high-energy composition. Viviers has a gift for injecting outsized personality into modest canvases, and Dragon Kitties exemplifies that approach: the diminutive scale belies real warmth and detail, with the subject combination carrying the playful irreverence that runs through much of the Pretoria-based artist's recent work. Part of a productive 2025 body of work that sees Viviers pushing into new subject territory, this original acrylic is available for acquisition.

artworks.title: Deadpool
artworks.description: Deadpool is a 20 × 20 cm acrylic on canvas completed in 2026. Walt Viviers has long drawn on comic book culture and popular iconography as raw material for his fine art practice, and this recent work channels the anarchic energy of Marvel's Deadpool into a concentrated, expressive painting. The square format suits the subject's punchy visual identity — Viviers' bold colour work and confident brushwork translate the character's graphic origins into something distinctly painterly, occupying the space where pop culture and fine art practice converge. One of Viviers' most recent works, Deadpool reflects the Pretoria-based South African artist's continued appetite for culturally charged subject matter. Enquire for acquisition details.

artworks.title: Duckbill
artworks.description: Duckbill is one of Walt Viviers' larger 2025 canvases, measuring 61 × 46 cm in acrylic on canvas. The platypus — one of nature's more improbable creatures — makes for an unexpectedly compelling portrait subject, and Viviers renders it with the same direct, personality-led approach he applies to all his animal work. The generous canvas size allows for a presence and level of detail that rewards viewing up close; the animal's unusual anatomy translated into bold, confident form. Based in Pretoria, Viviers has built a reputation for finding authentic character in animal subjects that others might overlook. Duckbill is a standout piece in his 2025 collection. Enquire for acquisition details.

artworks.title: Rat
artworks.description: Rat is a 51 × 40 cm acrylic on canvas from 2025. Walt Viviers approaches subjects that others might dismiss — the rat is a case in point, a creature more often associated with urban grime than gallery walls. But in Viviers' hands the animal is rendered with directness and a genuine sense of character: observed closely, executed with the assured brushwork that marks his mature style, and given the scale it deserves. The medium-large format ensures the subject has real presence. This is Viviers at his most straightforwardly honest — no irony, no distancing, just a Pretoria-based South African fine artist looking hard at a living thing and putting it on canvas. Enquire for acquisition details.

artworks.title: Sabretooth
artworks.description: Sabretooth is a 2025 acrylic on canvas by Walt Viviers. The sabre-toothed predator — whether drawn from the prehistoric Smilodon or the iconic Marvel villain — carries a raw, dangerous energy that suits Viviers' bold, direct painting style. His approach to animal and character subjects relies on decisive mark-making, strong form, and expressive colour; Sabretooth brings all of those qualities to bear on a subject that is inherently powerful. A striking addition to Viviers' 2025 body of work, the painting demonstrates the Pretoria-based South African artist's ability to find the emotional core of a subject and transfer it directly to canvas. The original is available for acquisition.

artworks.title: Crow
artworks.description: Crow is a 30 × 24 cm acrylic on canvas completed in 2025. The crow — a bird long associated with intelligence, mystery, and the darker edges of folklore — is a fitting subject for Viviers, who has consistently found genuine depth in the animal world. Painted in a compact format that concentrates the subject's presence, Crow is focused and atmospheric: the bird rendered with care and a sense of stillness that contrasts with the intensity of its gaze. Walt Viviers is a Pretoria-based South African fine artist whose animal paintings have been widely shared and are among his most collected works. This original acrylic on canvas is available for acquisition.

artworks.title: Rex
artworks.description: Rex is a 40 × 40 cm acrylic on canvas from 2025. The square format is one Viviers returns to often for animal portraits — its symmetry suits the directness of his approach. Rex is a personality-led painting: the subject rendered with the kind of attention and warmth that turns an animal portrait into a genuine character study. Viviers' mature brushwork is confident and economical, giving the canvas a presence that holds at a distance as well as it rewards up close. The Pretoria-based South African artist has developed a distinctive voice in animal painting — Rex is a strong example of it. The original is available for acquisition.

artworks.title: T-Rex
artworks.description: The T-Rex is one of Walt Viviers' most recognised works — a 78 × 38 cm acrylic on canvas that captures the primal force of the Tyrannosaurus rex with commanding scale and bold, expressive brushwork. Painted in 2020, it sits at the intersection of Viviers' interest in the animal world and his appetite for subjects that carry cultural weight. The unusually elongated horizontal format gives the predator a sweeping momentum, as if the canvas itself is barely containing the subject. Widely shared across social media since its release, T-Rex helped cement the Pretoria-based South African artist's reputation as a painter who brings genuine intensity to iconic subjects. It remains one of the signature pieces in Viviers' portfolio. The original is available for acquisition.

artworks.title: Venom Cat
artworks.description: Venom Cat is one of Walt Viviers' most popular works — a 20 × 20 cm acrylic on canvas completed in 2019. The painting merges the instantly recognisable aesthetic of Marvel's Venom with a cat, a combination that proved immediately compelling when first shared online. Featured in an interview with Pixelsmith Studios, Venom Cat helped establish Viviers as a South African fine artist with a distinctive voice: one that moves fluidly between painterly technique and popular culture without sacrificing either. The small square format packs considerable visual punch, the subject's high-contrast markings lending themselves naturally to Viviers' bold, confident brushwork. The original is available for acquisition.

artworks.title: Apocalypse Meow!
artworks.description: Apocalypse Meow! is a 26 × 21 cm ink and pencils on paper work from 2017, presented framed. One of Viviers' most recognisable earlier pieces, the work depicts a cat in an apocalyptic encounter — carrying the dark humour and raw energy that would come to define his reputation. Ink and pencil on paper allows for a level of immediacy distinct from his acrylic practice: gestural, spontaneous, and full of expressive line quality. Featured in a Pixelsmith Studios interview alongside Venom Cat, Apocalypse Meow! is among the works that first brought Walt Viviers wider attention as a Pretoria-based South African artist navigating the space between fine art and popular culture. Enquire for acquisition details.
